text: Sun in the 1st house: strong vitality, leadership, and self-confidence. Native has commanding presence and pride. Health is robust though prone to heat-related issues. Ego must be balanced to avoid arrogance.
Sun in the 2nd house: earns through government, authority, or father's connections. Speech is authoritative. Family life may see ego clashes. Wealth grows steadily through own effort and status.
Sun in the 3rd house: courageous, ambitious, and self-made. Success through own initiative and communication. Younger siblings may hold status. Good for media, writing, and short travels.
Sun in the 4th house: prestige through property and homeland, but domestic peace may suffer from authority conflicts. Relationship with mother needs care. Government-related property gains possible.
Sun in the 5th house: intelligent, creative, with leadership in education or speculation. Children may be few but accomplished. Good for politics, teaching, and performing arts.

Sun in the 6th house: victorious over enemies and competition. Excellent for service in government, medicine, or law enforcement. Strong immunity though digestive fire needs balance. Success in litigation.

Sun in the 7th house: spouse is dignified, proud, or authoritative; marriage may involve ego friction requiring mutual respect. Business partnerships with prominent people. Public dealings bring recognition but also rivalry.
Sun in the 8th house: interest in occult, research, and hidden matters. Father's longevity or relationship may be affected. Sudden changes in status. Health requires attention to chronic conditions.
Sun in the 9th house: fortunate, dharmic, and respected. Father is influential in native's life. Success in higher education, law, and religious pursuits. Government favor and long-distance travel.

Sun in the 10th house: natural leadership and authority in career. Native rises to positions of power in government, administration, or management. Father shapes professional life. Fame and recognition are strongly indicated.

Sun in the 11th house: gains through government, authority figures, and influential networks. Elder siblings prosper. Ambitions are fulfilled progressively with age. Income from status-based positions.
Sun in the 12th house: expenditure on noble causes, spiritual inclination, success in foreign lands. Government-related losses possible. Eyesight needs care. Solitude suits the native's growth.

Moon in the 1st house: emotional, intuitive, and popular personality. Changeable moods influence life direction. Attractive appearance and public appeal. Strong connection with mother shapes identity.

Moon in the 2nd house: wealth fluctuates like tides but family support is nurturing. Sweet, pleasing speech. Earnings through liquids, food, dairy, or public-facing trades. Attachment to family wealth.
Moon in the 3rd house: imaginative communicator, emotionally close to siblings. Courage fluctuates with mood. Good for writing, arts, and travel-related work. Mind benefits from creative expression.
Moon in the 4th house: deeply attached to home, mother, and homeland. Domestic happiness and comforts are strong. Gains from property and vehicles. Emotional security depends on family harmony.

Moon in the 5th house: creative, romantic, emotionally invested in children and education. Fertile imagination aids arts and speculation, though emotional investing needs caution. Affectionate bond with children.
Moon in the 6th house: emotional health tied to daily routine and service. Mother's health may need attention. Mind can worry over work and debts. Healing professions suit the native.
Moon in the 7th house: spouse is nurturing, emotional, and attractive. Native seeks emotional fulfillment through partnership. Marriage to a caring partner; public popularity in business dealings.

Moon in the 8th house: deep emotional intensity, psychic sensitivity, and interest in mysteries. Sudden emotional upheavals. Gains through inheritance possible. Mother's health or relationship sees fluctuations.
Moon in the 9th house: devoted, fortunate, and emotionally connected to faith and philosophy. Blessings of mother and teachers. Foreign travel and higher learning bring emotional growth.

Moon in the 10th house: career in public-facing fields — service, hospitality, food, healthcare, or public relations. Reputation fluctuates but popularity aids professional rise. Emotionally invested in work.
Moon in the 11th house: gains through women, public networks, and popular ventures. Many friends and social fulfillment. Desires are progressively achieved. Income from public-oriented business.
Moon in the 12th house: introspective, dreamy, drawn to solitude and spirituality. Success in foreign lands or behind-the-scenes work. Sleep and mental peace need protection. Charitable heart.

Mars in the 1st house (Manglik): energetic, courageous, independent, but impulsive and quick-tempered. Strong physique with risk of injuries. Manglik dosha considered for marriage matching. Natural fighter spirit.
Mars in the 2nd house (Manglik): sharp or harsh speech, family disputes possible. Earns through technical skill, engineering, or land. Wealth comes with effort; impulsive spending needs control.
Mars in the 3rd house: exceptionally courageous and enterprising. Bold communicator, may dominate siblings. Success through initiative, sports, defense, or technical writing. Wins competitions.

Mars in the 4th house (Manglik): domestic friction or property disputes possible. Gains land and vehicles through own effort. Mother's health needs care. Home environment carries tension needing conscious peace-making.

Mars in the 5th house: passionate, competitive intelligence. Speculative risks need discipline. Children may be spirited; first pregnancy needs care. Good for sports, engineering education, and strategy.
Mars in the 6th house: crushes enemies and wins disputes. Excellent for military, police, surgery, and competitive fields. Strong recovery from illness. Debts are cleared through aggressive effort.

Mars in the 7th house (Manglik): passionate but combative approach to partnership. Spouse is energetic and strong-willed; conflicts need patience. Marriage matching with Manglik chart traditionally advised. Business partnerships face friction.

Mars in the 8th house (Manglik): interest in surgery, research, and occult. Sudden events and accidents need caution. Longevity of relationships tested. Inheritance disputes possible. Transformative willpower.
Mars in the 9th house: champion of beliefs, may clash with father or teachers over ideology. Fortune through courage and technical fields. Adventurous travels. Dharma pursued with warrior spirit.

Mars in the 10th house: dynamic, ambitious career in engineering, defense, surgery, real estate, or leadership roles. Rises through decisive action. Known for execution power. Conflicts with authority possible.
Mars in the 11th house: gains through courage, technical ventures, and influential friends. Ambitions achieved through aggressive pursuit. Elder sibling relationship is dynamic. Strong earning drive.

Mars in the 12th house (Manglik): energy spent in foreign lands, hospitals, or isolation. Hidden enemies need vigilance. Impulsive expenses. Sexual energy strong. Spiritual discipline channels the fire well.
Mercury in the 1st house: intelligent, witty, youthful appearance and adaptable personality. Skilled in communication, mathematics, and commerce. Learns quickly. Nervous energy needs grounding.

Mercury in the 2nd house: earns through intellect, writing, accounting, trade, or speech-based professions. Articulate and persuasive speaker. Family values education. Multiple income sources possible.
Mercury in the 3rd house: brilliant communicator and writer. Close intellectual bond with siblings. Success in media, publishing, sales, and short travels. Restless, curious mind.

Mercury in the 4th house: educated homely environment, gains through property documentation or vehicles trade. Good relationship with mother. Home doubles as workplace or study. Emotional intelligence.

Mercury in the 5th house: sharp analytical intelligence, excellent for mathematics, astrology, and speculation with calculation. Children are intelligent. Creative writing and teaching talent. Romantic communication.
Mercury in the 6th house: analytical problem-solver in service. Success in law, accounting, medicine, or detailed work. Nervous system needs care under stress. Wins arguments through logic.

Mercury in the 7th house: spouse is intelligent, communicative, youthful, possibly met through work or education. Business acumen in partnerships. Marriage thrives on intellectual connection and conversation.
Mercury in the 8th house: penetrating research intellect, interest in occult sciences, taxation, insurance, or forensics. Gains through in-laws possible. Secretive communication. Longevity is good.
Mercury in the 9th house: scholarly, philosophical mind, excels in higher education and publishing. Fortune through intellect and teaching. Multiple areas of study. Travels for learning.

Mercury in the 10th house: career in communication, commerce, writing, analytics, technology, or consulting. Versatile professional skills. Reputation for intelligence. Multiple career streams possible.
Mercury in the 11th house: gains through intellect, trade, networking, and communication ventures. Wide social circle of learned friends. Multiple income streams. Business networking brings profits.

Mercury in the 12th house: imaginative, intuitive intellect suited for research, foreign work, or spiritual study. Expenses on education and travel. Quiet analytical work behind the scenes suits best.
Jupiter in the 1st house: wise, optimistic, respected personality with natural dignity. Protective blessings on life. Inclined to teaching and guiding. Healthy constitution; weight gain with age.
Jupiter in the 2nd house: wealth grows through wisdom, teaching, finance, or counsel. Truthful, refined speech. Prosperous family values. Savings accumulate steadily; generous with family.
Jupiter in the 3rd house: wise counsel to siblings, courage guided by ethics. Success in publishing, teaching, and communication of knowledge. Fortunate short journeys. Optimistic effort.
Jupiter in the 4th house: domestic happiness, large or auspicious home, blessings of mother. Gains through property and education. Vehicles and comforts increase. Peaceful heart.

Jupiter in the 5th house: blessed with intelligent, virtuous children and excellent education. Strong purva punya (past merit). Success in teaching, advisory, and ethical speculation. Devotional creativity.
Jupiter in the 6th house: protection in disputes and illness; enemies are forgiven or transformed. Service in law, healing, or counseling. Health guarded but liver and weight need moderation.
Jupiter in the 7th house: spouse is wise, cultured, and spiritually inclined. Marriage brings fortune and growth. Partnerships prosper through fairness. Respected in public dealings.
Jupiter in the 8th house: gains through inheritance, insurance, or spouse's wealth. Deep interest in metaphysics and hidden knowledge. Longevity is protected. Transformation through wisdom.
Jupiter in the 9th house: highly fortunate and dharmic; Jupiter is strong here. Blessings of gurus and father. Success in higher education, law, religion, and long travels. Faith guides destiny.

Jupiter in the 10th house: strong career in teaching, law, finance, or advisory roles. Native gains respect and authority in profession. Ethical reputation. Recognition from institutions and government.

Jupiter in the 11th house: abundant gains, fulfilled ambitions, and influential noble friends. Income expands through wisdom-based ventures. Elder siblings are supportive. Wealth accumulates with age.
Jupiter in the 12th house: spiritual wisdom, generous charity, and moksha inclination. Success and comfort in foreign lands. Expenses on good causes. Protection in difficult times through grace.
Venus in the 1st house: charming, attractive, artistic personality with refined tastes. Loved by others. Comforts and luxuries come naturally. Balanced, diplomatic approach to life.
Venus in the 2nd house: wealth through arts, beauty, luxury goods, or entertainment. Sweet speech and attractive face. Family life is comfortable with refined possessions and good food.
Venus in the 3rd house: artistic communication, harmonious sibling bonds. Talent in music, writing, and design. Pleasant short travels. Courage expressed through charm rather than force.
Venus in the 4th house: beautiful home, luxurious vehicles, and domestic bliss. Loving mother. Gains through property and comforts. Artistic home environment. Deep appreciation of beauty.
Venus in the 5th house: romantic, artistic, creatively gifted. Love affairs are prominent. Children are attractive and artistic. Success in entertainment, arts, and pleasurable ventures.

Venus in the 6th house: service in beauty, fashion, hospitality, or wellness industries. Relationship harmony requires effort. Health of reproductive system and kidneys needs care. Charm defuses conflicts.
Venus in the 7th house: Venus in own natural house — spouse is beautiful, artistic, and devoted. Marriage is central to happiness. Harmonious partnerships in business. Strong romantic nature.
Venus in the 8th house: gains through spouse, inheritance, or joint finances. Magnetic attraction. Interest in hidden pleasures and tantra. Relationships undergo deep transformations.
Venus in the 9th house: fortunate through spouse and arts. Refined philosophy, love of culture and beauty in higher learning. Foreign travel for pleasure. Devotional arts bring grace.
Venus in the 10th house: career in arts, entertainment, luxury, fashion, hospitality, or diplomacy. Charming professional reputation. Success through social grace. Popularity aids rise.
Venus in the 11th house: gains through arts, women, luxury ventures, and social networks. Influential charming friends. Desires for comfort are fulfilled. Wealth through aesthetic ventures.
Venus in the 12th house: pleasure in solitude, luxury in foreign lands, secret romances possible. Strong bed comforts. Generous spending on pleasures. Spiritual devotion through love and art.
Saturn in the 1st house: serious, disciplined, mature personality; life improves steadily after early struggles. Lean constitution. Responsibility shapes identity. Patience is the greatest strength.
Saturn in the 2nd house: wealth accumulates slowly through persistent effort and savings. Speech is measured or blunt. Family responsibilities are heavy. Financial security comes after 35.

Saturn in the 3rd house: disciplined courage and methodical effort. Relationships with siblings carry duty. Success in structured communication, engineering documentation, or long-term projects. Perseverance wins.
Saturn in the 4th house: early domestic hardship or distance from mother; property comes late but is lasting. Home carries responsibility. Emotional discipline develops. Old properties or land gains.

Saturn in the 5th house: delayed or limited children; serious approach to education and romance. Disciplined intellect suited to research. Speculation brings losses — avoid gambling. Late-blooming creativity.

Saturn in the 6th house: excellent placement — defeats enemies through persistence. Strong in service, law, and labor management. Chronic ailments are managed with discipline. Debts cleared systematically.
Saturn in the 7th house: marriage delayed, often after 28-30. Spouse is mature, responsible, possibly older. Marriage stabilizes and endures after initial obstacles. Commitments are taken seriously.
Saturn in the 8th house: long life through endurance. Chronic health matters need management. Inheritance is delayed or disputed. Deep karmic lessons through crises. Research and occult discipline.
Saturn in the 9th house: tested faith, practical philosophy. Father relationship carries distance or duty. Fortune rises slowly through ethical hard work. Traditional learning suits the native.

Saturn in the 10th house: Saturn is strong here — career built through relentless discipline reaches lasting authority. Rise is slow but position becomes unshakeable. Leadership in structured organizations, administration, or industry.

Saturn in the 11th house: gains accumulate steadily and grow large with age. Elder friends and disciplined networks bring profit. Ambitions fulfilled late but permanently. Income from long-term ventures.

Saturn in the 12th house: expenses require discipline; success in foreign lands through hard work. Solitude and spiritual austerity suit the native. Sleep needs care. Service to the suffering brings growth.

Rahu in the 1st house: unconventional, ambitious personality with magnetic yet mysterious presence. Strong desire for recognition. Identity evolves dramatically through life. Foreign influence on self-image.
Rahu in the 2nd house: wealth through unconventional, foreign, or technology-driven sources. Speech may be unusual or influential. Family circumstances are non-traditional. Sudden financial changes.
Rahu in the 3rd house: extraordinary courage and bold ambition. Success in media, technology, and daring ventures. Unusual sibling dynamics. Master of communication strategy and self-promotion.

Rahu in the 4th house: unconventional home life; may settle far from birthplace or abroad. Restless domestic mind. Gains through property technology or foreign real estate. Mother's influence is complex.
Rahu in the 5th house: unconventional intelligence and creative obsessions. Speculative gains and losses can be dramatic. Children's matters may be unusual or delayed. Romance breaks traditions.

Rahu in the 6th house: excellent for defeating enemies and competition through strategy. Success in litigation, technology services, and foreign employment. Unusual health issues that are hard to diagnose but manageable.

Rahu in the 7th house: unconventional marriage — spouse may be foreign, from different background, or unusual profession. Intense partnership desires. Business with foreign connections. Relationships transform the native.
Rahu in the 8th house: deep obsession with mysteries, research, and transformation. Sudden gains or losses through others' wealth. Unusual longevity patterns. Occult abilities; secretive nature.
Rahu in the 9th house: unconventional beliefs, foreign philosophy, breaks from tradition. Fortune through foreign connections. Guru relationships are unusual. Long journeys transform worldview.
Rahu in the 10th house: intense ambition for status and worldly success. Career in foreign companies, technology, or unconventional fields. Sudden rises possible. Guard against unethical shortcuts.

Rahu in the 11th house: massive gains through networks, technology, and foreign sources — one of Rahu's best placements. Influential unusual friends. Ambitions achieved on large scale. Income multiplies suddenly.

Rahu in the 12th house: strong foreign settlement indication. Expenses can spiral without discipline. Interest in hidden knowledge and altered states. Spiritual liberation through unconventional paths.

Ketu in the 1st house: detached, spiritual, enigmatic personality. Self-identity feels elusive; headless pursuit of goals until spiritual purpose is found. Intuitive and psychic. Health of head needs care.
Ketu in the 2nd house: detachment from family wealth and accumulation. Speech is cryptic or spiritually inclined. Past-life mastery of resources. Non-attachment brings unexpected sufficiency.
Ketu in the 3rd house: intuitive courage without need for recognition. Detached from siblings. Effortless communication skills from past merit. Spiritual writing talent. Silent strength.
Ketu in the 4th house: detachment from home and homeland; inner peace sought over material comfort. May live away from birthplace. Mother connection is karmic. Spiritual home environment.
Ketu in the 5th house: intuitive, past-life intelligence; effortless grasp of spiritual and technical subjects. Detachment in romance. Children's matters carry karmic lessons. Mantra siddhi possible.
Ketu in the 6th house: enemies dissolve without effort — excellent placement. Healing abilities, especially alternative medicine. Mysterious ailments resolve spiritually. Service without ego.

Ketu in the 7th house: detachment in marriage; spouse is spiritual or unusual. Partnership teaches non-attachment. Marriage needs conscious emotional investment to thrive. Business partnerships are karmic.
Ketu in the 8th house: natural mystic with deep occult insight — Ketu is comfortable here. Sudden intuitive transformations. Longevity through detachment. Research into hidden truths succeeds.
Ketu in the 9th house: born philosopher with past-life spiritual attainment. Questions traditions to find deeper truth. Pilgrimages transform. Guru appears when ego dissolves.
Ketu in the 10th house: detachment from career status; work feels karmically driven rather than ambition-driven. Success in spiritual, healing, research, or technical niches. Fame without seeking it.
Ketu in the 11th house: detachment from gains and social climbing; profits arrive unexpectedly when unattached. Few but spiritually meaningful friendships. Desires dissolve into contentment.

Ketu in the 12th house: strongest moksha placement — deep meditation ability and liberation-oriented soul. Foreign or ashram life suits. Expenses on spirituality. Enlightenment potential through surrender.

Sun Mahadasha (6 years): period of authority, recognition, and government favor if Sun is strong. Career advancement and clarity of purpose. Father-related events. If afflicted: ego conflicts and health heat issues.

Moon Mahadasha (10 years): emotional and domestic focus. Public popularity, travel, and mother-related events. Mind-driven decisions. If Moon is strong: comfort and gains; if afflicted: emotional turbulence.

Mars Mahadasha (7 years): energetic action period. Property acquisition, courage, and competitive wins. Technical career growth. If afflicted: accidents, disputes, and impulsive decisions need control.

Mercury Mahadasha (17 years): intellectual and commercial growth. Education, business expansion, communication success, and new skills. Networking flourishes. If afflicted: nervous stress and scattered efforts.

Jupiter Mahadasha (16 years): generally auspicious — growth in knowledge, wealth, family, and spirituality. Marriage, childbirth, and higher education are common. Blessings of teachers support the native.

Venus Mahadasha (20 years): longest dasha — comforts, luxury, marriage, vehicles, and artistic success. Relationships take center stage. If Venus is strong: prosperity; if afflicted: relationship and indulgence lessons.

Saturn Mahadasha (19 years): discipline, hard work, and karmic lessons. Rewards come slowly but last. Well-placed Saturn brings career consolidation, property, and authority; afflicted brings delays and responsibility tests.

Rahu Mahadasha (18 years): ambitious, transformative period of worldly pursuit. Foreign opportunities, technology gains, and sudden rises. Material desires intensify. Ethical grounding prevents downfall; unconventional paths open doors.

Ketu Mahadasha (7 years): detachment and spiritual awakening period. Material matters feel less satisfying; introspection deepens. Sudden endings clear karmic debts. Meditation and letting go bring peace and intuitive gifts.
